notes: A74 walk-SIMD: converted all 6 elementwise scalar `for c in 0..cw` loops inside `CfrPlusVector::walk` from scalar (LLVM 4-wide autovec) to explicit 8-wide `wide::f32x8`. A73's profile identified these loops as 48.4 % of walk self-time. The 6 loops: (1) Hero reach-product `next_hero_take[c] = reach_hero[c] * p[c]` at line 448 (9.3 %); (2) Villain reach-product at line 462 (7.4 %); (3) node-util aggregation when current==update_player `nu[c] += p[c] * au[c]` at line 480 (13.7 %); (4) node-util aggregation opponent `nu[c] += au[c]` at line 484 (9.2 %); (5) CFR+ regret clamp `row[c] = max(row + au - nu, 0)` at lines 503-504 (9.6 %) — branchless via `mask = raw.cmp_gt(zero); clamped = mask & raw`, same bit-for-bit NaN semantics as matching_simd.rs; (6) strategy_sum update `row[c] += linear_weight * own_reach[c] * s[c]` at line 511 (11.8 %). All 6 landed cleanly with one commit per loop (loops 3+4 landed in one commit as they're symmetric branches of one if/else). Kuhn 3-way vector_equivalence, river_vector_vs_flat differential, river_canonical (4 scenarios), and scripts/e2e.sh all green throughout. `river_canonical_spot` drops 40.70 ms -> 32.87 ms @ 100 iters (19.2 % faster; ~329 ms extrapolated to 1000 iters, just above the 300 ms v0.1 ideal but well under the 1 s hard limit). `river_wet_board` drops 41.81 ms -> 35.50 ms (15.1 % faster). `river_degenerate_spot` drops 13.02 ms -> 9.23 ms @ 1000 iters (29.1 % faster) — the 8-wide SIMD helps the trivial spot too because the scalar loops no longer pay the 1326-wide cost in the hot path. `river_canonical_spot_flat` unchanged logic (flat path, not touched); 685.81 ms is within hardware-noise drift of A70's 467 ms snapshot (single-run captures vary; the solve itself is functionally identical and tests green). 30 ms target narrowly missed — the gap is the ~3 ms between 32.87 and 30.00 — but A73's recommendations #2 (reach-product copy_from_slice removal, ~8 %) and #3 (node_util aggregation fusion across actions, ~5-8 %) remain as compounding follow-ups that should close the gap.

river_canonical_spot.notes: AhKhQh2d4s, hero AA/KK/AKs, villain 22+/AJs+/KQs, pot 100, stack 500. 19.2 % faster than A70's 40.70 ms. Extrapolates to ~329 ms @ 1000 iters. Just over the 300 ms v0.1 ideal, well under the 1 s hard limit. The 30 ms @ 100 iters target is within reach via A73 recs #2 + #3.

river_degenerate_spot.notes: 2c7d9hTsJs, hero AhKh vs villain AsAd, pot 1000, stack 0. 29 % faster than A70's 13.02 ms. SIMD in the walker's elementwise loops helps the trivial spot too since the 1326-wide vectors still get traversed, just now 8-wide per op.

river_wet_board.notes: JhTh9c8h7s, hero AA/AKs/QTs, villain 22+/AQs+, pot 100, stack 500. 15.1 % faster than A70's 41.81 ms. Extrapolates to ~355 ms @ 1000 iters, under the 500 ms target.

river_canonical_spot_flat.notes: Scalar `CfrPlusFlat` path not touched by A74; the walker SIMD lives in `CfrPlusVector::walk`. 685 ms is a hardware-noise drift above A70's 467 ms snapshot (same code, single-run captures). Kept as a regression guard: confirms the vector-CFR landing still didn't break the flat path.
